Function: nnp_calc_energy_force Module: cp2k.psmp Source: nnp_force.F:62-319 [...] Coverage (incl. loops): 0.83% | (excl. loops): 0.04%

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


62:    SUBROUTINE nnp_calc_energy_force(nnp, calc_forces)


[...]


75:       TYPE(atomic_kind_type), DIMENSION(:), POINTER      :: atomic_kind_set


76:       TYPE(cp_logger_type), POINTER                      :: logger


77:       TYPE(cp_subsys_type), POINTER                      :: subsys


78:       TYPE(distribution_1d_type), POINTER                :: local_particles


79:       TYPE(particle_type), DIMENSION(:), POINTER         :: particle_set


80:       TYPE(section_vals_type), POINTER                   :: print_key, print_section


81:       TYPE(virial_type), POINTER                         :: virial


82: 


83:       CALL timeset(routineN, handle)


84: 


85:       NULLIFY (particle_set, logger, local_particles, subsys, &


86:                atomic_kind_set)


87:       logger => cp_get_default_logger()


88: 


89:       CPASSERT(ASSOCIATED(nnp))


90:       CALL nnp_env_get(nnp_env=nnp, particle_set=particle_set, &


91:                        subsys=subsys, local_particles=local_particles, &


92:                        atomic_kind_set=atomic_kind_set)


93: 


94:       CALL cp_subsys_get(subsys, &


95:                          virial=virial)


96: 


97:       calc_stress = virial%pv_availability .AND. (.NOT. virial%pv_numer)


98:       IF (calc_stress .AND. .NOT. calc_forces) CPABORT('Stress cannot be calculated without forces')


[...]


104:       need_per_member = nnp%bias


105:       print_section => section_vals_get_subs_vals(nnp%nnp_input, "PRINT")


106:       print_key => section_vals_get_subs_vals(print_section, "FORCES")


107:       IF (BTEST(cp_print_key_should_output(logger%iter_info, print_key), cp_p_file)) &


108:          need_per_member = .TRUE.


109:       print_key => section_vals_get_subs_vals(print_section, "FORCES_SIGMA")


110:       IF (BTEST(cp_print_key_should_output(logger%iter_info, print_key), cp_p_file)) &


111:          need_per_member = .TRUE.


112: 


113:       ! Initialize energy and gradient:


114:       nnp%atomic_energy(:, :) = 0.0_dp


115:       IF (calc_forces) nnp%myforce(:, :, :) = 0.0_dp


116:       IF (calc_forces .AND. need_per_member) nnp%committee_forces(:, :, :) = 0.0_dp


117:       IF (calc_stress) nnp%committee_stress(:, :, :) = 0.0_dp


[...]


123:       IF (.NOT. nnp%sort_initialized) THEN


124:          ig = 1


125:          DO i = 1, nnp%n_ele


126:             DO j = 1, nnp%num_atoms


127:                IF (nnp%ele(i) == particle_set(j)%atomic_kind%element_symbol) THEN


128:                   nnp%atoms(ig) = nnp%ele(i)


129:                   CALL get_ptable_info(nnp%atoms(ig), number=nnp%nuc_atoms(ig))


130:                   nnp%ele_ind(ig) = i


131:                   nnp%sort(ig) = j


132:                   nnp%sort_inv(j) = ig


133:                   ig = ig + 1


134:                END IF


135:             END DO


136:          END DO


137:          nnp%sort_initialized = .TRUE.


138:       END IF


139: 


140:       !fill coord array


141:       DO ig = 1, nnp%num_atoms


142:          DO m = 1, 3


143:             nnp%coord(m, ig) = particle_set(nnp%sort(ig))%r(m)


[...]


150:       n_base = nnp%num_atoms/logger%para_env%num_pe


151:       n_remainder = MOD(nnp%num_atoms, logger%para_env%num_pe)


152:       IF (logger%para_env%mepos < n_remainder) THEN


153:          mecalc = n_base + 1


154:          istart = 1 + logger%para_env%mepos*(n_base + 1)


155:       ELSE


156:          mecalc = n_base


157:          istart = 1 + n_remainder*(n_base + 1) + (logger%para_env%mepos - n_remainder)*n_base


158:       END IF


159: 


160:       ! reset extrapolation status


161:       nnp%output_expol = .FALSE.


162: 


163:       ! Build (or refresh) the cell list once per force evaluation; the


164:       ! subsequent nnp_compute_neighbors calls walk only the bins around the


165:       ! centre atom.


166:       CALL nnp_prepare_cell_list_cache(nnp)


[...]


172:       IF (calc_forces) THEN


173:          max_nsym = 0


174:          DO ie = 1, nnp%n_ele


175:             IF (nnp%arc(ie)%n_nodes(1) > max_nsym) max_nsym = nnp%arc(ie)%n_nodes(1)


176:          END DO


177:          ! Reallocate only on first call or when the system size changes.


178:          IF (.NOT. ALLOCATED(dsymdxyz) .OR. SIZE(dsymdxyz, 3) /= nnp%num_atoms) THEN


179:             IF (ALLOCATED(dsymdxyz)) THEN


180:                DEALLOCATE (dsymdxyz, active_atoms, active_flag, denergydsym)


181:                IF (ALLOCATED(stress)) DEALLOCATE (stress)


182:             END IF


183:             ALLOCATE (dsymdxyz(max_nsym, 3, nnp%num_atoms), SOURCE=0.0_dp)


184:             ALLOCATE (active_atoms(nnp%num_atoms))


185:             ALLOCATE (active_flag(nnp%num_atoms), SOURCE=0)


186:             ALLOCATE (denergydsym(max_nsym))


187:          END IF


188:          IF (calc_stress .AND. .NOT. ALLOCATED(stress)) ALLOCATE (stress(3, 3, max_nsym))


189:       END IF


190: 


191:       ! calc atomic contribution to energy and force


192:       DO i = istart, istart + mecalc - 1


193: 


194:          ! determine index of atom type and offset


195:          ind = nnp%ele_ind(i)


196: 


197:          ! reset input nodes of ele(ind):


198:          nnp%arc(ind)%layer(1)%node(:) = 0.0_dp


199: 


200:          ! compute sym fnct values


201:          IF (calc_forces) THEN


202:             !reset input grads of ele(ind):


203:             nnp%arc(ind)%layer(1)%node_grad(:) = 0.0_dp


204:             IF (calc_stress) THEN


205:                stress(:, :, 1:nnp%arc(ind)%n_nodes(1)) = 0.0_dp


206:                CALL nnp_calc_acsf(nnp, i, dsymdxyz, stress, &


207:                                   active_atoms=active_atoms, active_flag=active_flag, &


208:                                   n_active=n_active)


209:             ELSE


210:                CALL nnp_calc_acsf(nnp, i, dsymdxyz, &


211:                                   active_atoms=active_atoms, active_flag=active_flag, &


212:                                   n_active=n_active)


213:             END IF


214:          ELSE


215:             CALL nnp_calc_acsf(nnp, i)


216:          END IF


217: 


218:          DO i_com = 1, nnp%n_committee


219:             ! predict energy


220:             CALL nnp_predict(nnp%arc(ind), nnp, i_com)


221:             nnp%atomic_energy(i, i_com) = nnp%arc(ind)%layer(nnp%n_layer)%node(1) + &


222:                                           nnp%atom_energies(ind)


223: 


224:             ! predict forces; the inner loop is restricted to the active set


225:             ! built by nnp_calc_acsf, so the cost is O(n_active) per centre.


226:             IF (calc_forces) THEN


227:                ! nnp_gradients fully assigns denergydsym(1:n_nodes(1))


228:                CALL nnp_gradients(nnp%arc(ind), nnp, i_com, denergydsym)


229:                IF (calc_stress) THEN


230:                   DO j = 1, nnp%arc(ind)%n_nodes(1)


231:                      nnp%committee_stress(:, :, i_com) = nnp%committee_stress(:, :, i_com) - &


232:                                                          denergydsym(j)*stress(:, :, j)


233:                   END DO


234:                END IF


235:                DO idx = 1, n_active


236:                   k = active_atoms(idx)


237:                   DO m = 1, 3


238:                      nnp%myforce(m, k, i_com) = nnp%myforce(m, k, i_com) - &


239:                         DOT_PRODUCT(denergydsym(1:nnp%arc(ind)%n_nodes(1)), &


240:                                     dsymdxyz(1:nnp%arc(ind)%n_nodes(1), m, k))


241:                   END DO


242:                END DO


243:             END IF


244:          END DO


245: 


246:          ! Zero only the slices of dsymdxyz that this centre touched and


247:          ! reset active_flag for the next centre (O(n_active) work).


248:          IF (calc_forces) THEN


249:             DO idx = 1, n_active


250:                k = active_atoms(idx)


251:                dsymdxyz(1:nnp%arc(ind)%n_nodes(1), :, k) = 0.0_dp


252:                active_flag(k) = 0


253:             END DO


254:          END IF


255: 


256:       END DO ! loop over num_atoms


[...]


262:       nnp%committee_energy(:) = SUM(nnp%atomic_energy, 1)


263:       CALL logger%para_env%sum(nnp%committee_energy)


264:       nnp%nnp_potential_energy = SUM(nnp%committee_energy)/REAL(nnp%n_committee, dp)


265: 


266:       IF (calc_forces) THEN


267:          IF (need_per_member) THEN


268:             ! bring myforce to force array


269:             DO j = 1, nnp%num_atoms


270:                DO k = 1, 3


271:                   nnp%committee_forces(k, (nnp%sort(j)), :) = nnp%myforce(k, j, :)


272:                END DO


273:             END DO


274:             CALL logger%para_env%sum(nnp%committee_forces)


275:             nnp%nnp_forces(:, :) = SUM(nnp%committee_forces, DIM=3)/REAL(nnp%n_committee, dp)


276:          ELSE


277:             ! Average over the committee locally and reduce a single (3, N)


278:             ! array; committee_forces is left untouched.


279:             nnp%nnp_forces(:, :) = 0.0_dp


280:             DO i_com = 1, nnp%n_committee


281:                DO j = 1, nnp%num_atoms


282:                   nnp%nnp_forces(:, nnp%sort(j)) = nnp%nnp_forces(:, nnp%sort(j)) + &


283:                                                    nnp%myforce(:, j, i_com)


284:                END DO


285:             END DO


286:             nnp%nnp_forces(:, :) = nnp%nnp_forces(:, :)/REAL(nnp%n_committee, dp)


287:             CALL logger%para_env%sum(nnp%nnp_forces)


288:          END IF


289:          DO j = 1, nnp%num_atoms


290:             particle_set(j)%f(:) = nnp%nnp_forces(:, j)


291:          END DO


292:       END IF


293: 


294:       IF (calc_stress) THEN


295:          CALL logger%para_env%sum(nnp%committee_stress)


296:          virial%pv_virial = SUM(nnp%committee_stress, DIM=3)/REAL(nnp%n_committee, dp)


297:       END IF


298: 


299:       ! Bias the standard deviation of committee disagreement


300:       IF (nnp%bias) THEN


301:          CALL nnp_bias_sigma(nnp, calc_forces)


302:          nnp%nnp_potential_energy = nnp%nnp_potential_energy + nnp%bias_energy


303:          IF (calc_forces) THEN


304:             DO j = 1, nnp%num_atoms


305:                particle_set(j)%f(:) = particle_set(j)%f(:) + nnp%bias_forces(:, j)


306:             END DO


307:          END IF


308:          ! print properties if requested


309:          print_section => section_vals_get_subs_vals(nnp%nnp_input, "BIAS%PRINT")


310:          CALL nnp_print_bias(nnp, print_section)


311:       END IF


312: 


313:       ! print properties if requested


314:       print_section => section_vals_get_subs_vals(nnp%nnp_input, "PRINT")


315:       CALL nnp_print(nnp, print_section)


316: 


317:       CALL timestop(handle)


318: 


319:    END SUBROUTINE nnp_calc_energy_force

The code analyzed by CQA in that panel excludes loops and represents 0.04% of application time for run run_0
        

          
Source file and linesnnp_force.F:62-319

          
Modulecp2k.psmp

        

        
The function is defined in /home/crm98/cp2k_optimized/build/src/nnp_force.F:62,75-98,104-117,123-143,150-166,172-256,262-319.

Code clean check


Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation).
By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 507.00 to 45.00 cycles (11.27x speedup).

Workaround


  • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
  • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)
  • To reference allocatable arrays, use "allocatable" instead of "pointer" pointers or qualify them with the "contiguous" attribute (Fortran 2008)
  • For structures, limit to one indirection. For example, use a_b%c instead of a%b%c with a_b set to a%b before this function


Vectorization


Your function is probably not vectorized.
Only 12% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions).


Details


Store and arithmetical SSE/AVX instructions are used in scalar version (process only one data element in vector registers).


Workaround


Read the "512-bits vectorization" report at "Potential" confidence level.

Execution units bottlenecks


Found no such bottlenecks but see expert reports for more complex bottlenecks.


  


512-bits vectorization


On some x86 processors supporting 512-bits vectorization, compilers are often too conservative and limit vectorization to 256 bits. Performance can then be improved by enforcing 512-bits vectorization, especially with many vectorized and high trip count loops. 512-bits vectorization performance overhead (compared to 256-bits) is generally lower on newer processors.


Workaround


Recompile with -qopt-zmm-usage=high


  


CALL instructions


Detected function call instructions.


Details


Calling (and then returning from) a function prevents many compiler optimizations (like vectorization), breaks control flow (which reduces pipeline performance) and executes extra instructions to save/restore the registers used inside it, which is very expensive (dozens of cycles). Consider to inline small functions.

Complex instructions


Detected COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS.


Details


These instructions generate more than one micro-operation and only one of them can be decoded during a cycle and the extra micro-operations increase pressure on execution units.

Workaround


Avoid mixing data with different types. In particular, check if the type of constants is the same as array elements.

Type of elements and instruction set


12 SSE or AVX instructions are processing arithmetic or math operations on double precision FP elements in scalar mode (one at a time).

The binary function is composed of 12 FP arithmetical operations:

The binary function is storing 2306 bytes (288 double precision FP elements).

Assuming all data fit into the L1 cache, each call to the function takes 507.00 cycles. At this rate:

Code clean check


Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation).
By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 507.00 to 45.00 cycles (11.27x speedup).

Workaround


  • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
  • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)
  • To reference allocatable arrays, use "allocatable" instead of "pointer" pointers or qualify them with the "contiguous" attribute (Fortran 2008)
  • For structures, limit to one indirection. For example, use a_b%c instead of a%b%c with a_b set to a%b before this function


Vectorization


Your function is probably not vectorized.
Only 12% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions).


Details


Store and arithmetical SSE/AVX instructions are used in scalar version (process only one data element in vector registers).


Workaround


Read the "512-bits vectorization" report at "Potential" confidence level.

Execution units bottlenecks


Found no such bottlenecks but see expert reports for more complex bottlenecks.


  


512-bits vectorization


On some x86 processors supporting 512-bits vectorization, compilers are often too conservative and limit vectorization to 256 bits. Performance can then be improved by enforcing 512-bits vectorization, especially with many vectorized and high trip count loops. 512-bits vectorization performance overhead (compared to 256-bits) is generally lower on newer processors.


Workaround


Recompile with -qopt-zmm-usage=high


  


CALL instructions


Detected function call instructions.


Details


Calling (and then returning from) a function prevents many compiler optimizations (like vectorization), breaks control flow (which reduces pipeline performance) and executes extra instructions to save/restore the registers used inside it, which is very expensive (dozens of cycles). Consider to inline small functions.
